english: Ar. حقّه the hookah, with an inflexible tube.
W., حكم Urdu, order, command.
the sound of sighing Pth.
Cs.: Ssk. (hū-na) n. of an ancient people, the Huns (?).
(?) Sch.: title of the Chinese emperor.
mig hu-ré ¸dug (mig hu re 'dug) he stares, he goggles, with wonder, horror, confusion Mil., Glr.; mig-húr (mig hur) Mñg.
(from the Hind. hošyār?) (grown) well again, being again lively, active; diligent, sedulous W.
1. interj. expressive of pain from cold Cs. -- 2. 'the sound of one's mouth in eating' Cs. -- 3. *hu-hú tań-ce* (*hu hu tang ce) W. to whistle.
mystical interjection, e.g. in the prayer of six syllables, v. ཨོཾ་

W. (= ča (ca), lon (lon), ¸prin ('phrin)) news, tidings, intelligence, information, *hun tań-ce* (*hun tang ce) to give account or notice, to inform, acquaint, let know, *tsar-na hun toń* (*tshar na hun tong) tell me (let me know) as soon as it is finished! *hun tsór-ce* (*hun tshor ce) to get intelligence, to receive news; *hun ma yoń* (*hun ma yong) or *mi dug* (*mi 'dug) we have no news yet; disclosure, explanation, opinion, idea, *ci yód-pe hun k'yód-la juń yin* (*ci yod pa'i hun khyod la 'byung yin) you shall get an idea of what kind of ... are to be found, *sém-can ťun-ćan žig yin-pe hun ńá-la juń* (*sems can thun can zhig yin pa'i hun nga la 'byung) I have got the notion that this is a very quiet animal; *re-rëi hun ćil-tar šè* (*re re'i hun ci ltar shes) whence have you such accurate information of every one of them? *ser-dub dīl-te hun ma juń* (*gser gdub dril te hun ma 'byung) he did not perceive it when the ring fell off; *hun-méd-la* (*hun med la) unexpectedly, unawares.

as much as is swallowed at once, a gulp, a draught, tóg-mar húb re ¸fuń žig (thog mar hub re 'thung zhig) at first take only one mouthful, one draught at a time Glr.; hub yčig (hub gcig) one mouthful, hub do (hub do) two mouthfuls Cs.; húb-kyis (hub kyis) by draughts Sch.; hub-húb byéd-pa (hub hub byed pa) to drink in large draughts, to gulp.
1. v. hu-ré (hu re). -- 2. hur-húr (hur hur) v. had-had (had had).

dexterity, cleverness, skilfulness C., hur-tág (hur thag) id., also zeal, diligence Sch.; húr-po (hur po) 1. quick, alert, dexterous, clever. 2. hot, hasty, passionate Ld.; rta húr-po (rta hur po) a fleet, spirited, fine horse Cs.
Cs. moisture, humidity, hús-ćan (hus can) wet.
1. num.: 119. -- 2. interj.: o! holla! Cs.; he-he (he he) 1. id. Cs. 2. = ha-há (ha ha), he-hé (he he) zer bgád-pas (zer bgad pas) she laughed: he, he! Glr.
Ssk. cause, reason, argument, logic.
provocation, taunts, sarcasms.
Ssk. he-wajra (he ba dz+ra), ཀྱཻ་རྡོ་རྗེ་ Tibet. kye-rdo-rje (kye rdo rje) Cs.: n. of a god; n. of a series of treatises.
terrifying deities, also krag-ťuń (khrag 'thung), Thgr. frq., hé-ru-kai rgyud (he ru ka'i rgyud) legends of wrathful deities.
having become putrid, rancid.
Sch.: soft leather, wash-leather.
hél-po (hel po), hél-can (hel can), *hel-hél* (*hel hel) W. wide, extended; of garments: wide, easy; hél-ba (hel ba) id. and sbst.: width Sch.

Ssk. prop.: burnt-offering of butter; = sbyin-srég (sbyin sreg) v. Was. (194); Schl. 251; hó-ma byéd-pa (ho ma byed pa) to sacrifice; hom-kuń (hom khung) a small pit or a triangular box used as an altar for such an offering.
interj. of admiration Cs.
a medicinal herb, Picrorhiza, frequently to be found on the mountains, Hook. I., 272.
stupid, foolish Cs.
W.: *hób-te dug* (*hob te 'dug) it has got bent in or battered, of tin ware.
(Mongol word?) a pad, placed under a camel's load.
W. to fall away, to lose flesh, e.g. of hollow cheeks. to shrink, to shrivel, of withered fruit.
